user_message: Help me build renewal risk for VanArsdel Ltd. I think there's an opportunity here but need to validate.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-26 10:02 EST
Subject: Re: Re: renewal risk Timeline

There's been a change in our timeline for renewal risk. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
Date: 2024-02-06 15:15 EST
Subject: Re: renewal risk Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the renewal risk proposal. Our CTO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: renewal risk Discussion - VanArsdel Ltd
Date: 2024-02-12
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Kevin (VanArsdel Ltd)

[00:02:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on renewal risk from our last session.

[00:08:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

[00:11:00] Kevin (VanArsdel Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:16:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:18:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

[00:20:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:23:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Let me add some context -- we've been working on data residency and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-M trap (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-M failure mode (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct renewal risk decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-M -- Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
